summary: Lewis Hamilton arrives at Spa seeking to build momentum after his dominant Barcelona victory, potentially tilting Ferrari's internal confidence battle as the Scuderia searches for consistency against McLaren's resurgent pace. The wet-weather lottery of the Belgian circuit could amplify mid-field strategies, with Alpine and Haas teams primed to exploit unpredictable conditions that level the competitive hierarchy. A strong result for Hamilton would establish the narrative of a Ferrari revival around his arrival, while a McLaren triumph would cement their status as genuine title contenders and leave the Prancing Horse's championship hopes precariously balanced.
